D1 Optics L2 Understand Headliner

Why the Sky Is Blue

move the sun across the sky and watch Rayleigh scattering paint the daytime blue and the sunset red

Air molecules scatter shorter wavelengths far more strongly than longer ones — by a factor of 1/λ⁴ — leaving blue everywhere we don't look at the sun and red along the path that does.

Drag the sun across the sky. The colour of the zenith and the colour of the sun's direction update from the spectral integral of Rayleigh scattering over the air column.

Zenith colour
—
Sun-direction colour
—
Air path
—
